Title: VC1 catalyzes a key step in the biosynthesis of vicine in faba bean

Common_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Mélodie/2; low-vicine germplasm including Disco/1 and derived NILs
Common_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antinutritional compounds in faba bean seeds; implicated in favism in susceptible humans
Common_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Mutant allele of bifunctional RibA/RibB family protein VC1
Common_Genes.Subcellular_Localization: Likely chloroplast-targeted if translated, but protein is frameshifted/truncated; functional localization not established
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Expressed in seed coat cDNA of Mélodie/2, but as inactive frameshifted allele
Common_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Low-vicine lines specifically amplify vc1 allele in seed coat/genomic DNA
Common_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: vc1 is associated with the recessive low-vicine/low-convicine phenotype
Common_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Low-vicine cultivars harbor a vc1 allele carrying an AT insertion. 2. The insertion causes a frameshift within the GTP cyclohydrolase II domain and likely abolishes enzymatic function. 3. Presence of vc1 co-segregates with reduced vicine and convicine accumulation in mapping and NIL materials.
Common_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fine mapping; allele-specific PCR; cDNA/genomic sequencing; targeted metabolite profiling in segregants and NILs
Common_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: Useful diagnostic allele for marker-assisted breeding of low-vicine faba bean
Common_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, targeted metabolite profiling; NCBI BioProject PRJNA725986
Common_Genes.Other_Important_Info: vc1 is the low-vicine mutant allele of VC1 rather than a separate gene.

Pathway_Genes.Applied_Species_Variety: Hedin/2; Mélodie/2; Disco/1; Fabelle; Limbo_PC9.7901; NPZ1+/−; NPZ2+/−
Pathway_Genes.Terminal_Metabolite_Function: Antinutritional compounds in faba bean seeds; implicated in favism in susceptible humans
Pathway_Genes.Biosynthetic_Pathway: vicine/convicine biosynthesis linked to purine-derived branch from GTP; canonical riboflavin-biosynthesis-related step
Pathway_Genes.Pathway_Branch_or_Subpathway: GTP-derived entry step toward vicine/convicine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Enzyme_Name: GTP cyclohydrolase II / 3,4-dihydroxy-2-butanone-4-phosphate synthase bifunctional enzyme
Pathway_Genes.Protein_Family_or_Domain: Bifunctional RibA/RibB family protein with N-terminal chloroplast transit peptide, RibB domain, and RibA (GTP cyclohydrolase II) domain
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Source_Species: Highly expressed in whole seed at early filling and in seed coat; expression correlates strongly with vicine accumulation across tissues
Pathway_Genes.Expression_Pattern_of_Applied_Species: Seed coat/seed tissues; detectable in roots; higher active VC1 expression in high-vicine line Hedin/2 than low-vicine line Mélodie/2 seed coat
Pathway_Genes.Environment_or_Treatment_Factor: Field-grown and greenhouse-grown tissues; precursor feeding with labeled GTP in seedlings
Pathway_Genes.Catalyzed_Reaction_Description: Converts GTP to DARPP via the GTP cyclohydrolase II reaction; this provides the committed purine-derived precursor for vicine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.End_Product_Connection_Type: supplies direct precursor pool for vicine and convicine biosynthesis
Pathway_Genes.Rate_Limiting_or_Key_Control_Step: key control step supported; major determinant at the vc locus
Pathway_Genes.Core_Phenotypic_Effect: Functional VC1 promotes vicine and convicine accumulation; low-vicine vc1 allele with frameshift is associated with strongly reduced vicine/convicine levels
Pathway_Genes.Summary_Key_Findings_of_Core_Gene: 1. Fine mapping of the vc− locus identified VC1 as the candidate gene underlying low vicine/convicine phenotype. 2. VC1 expression across tissues was tightly correlated with vicine accumulation. 3. Low-vicine lines carry a vc1 allele with an AT insertion causing a frameshift in the GTP cyclohydrolase II domain. 4. Overexpression of VC1 in hairy roots increased vicine content. 5. Purified VC1 converted GTP to DARPP in enzyme assays, and isotope feeding with labeled GTP supported GTP as a vicine precursor.
Pathway_Genes.Core_Validation_Method: Fine mapping; gene-to-metabolite correlation; allele sequencing; ddPCR / allele-specific expression analysis; hairy-root overexpression; purified enzyme assay; stable-isotope precursor feeding; targeted metabolite profiling
Pathway_Genes.Breeding_or_Engineering_Application_Valu: VC1 is a prime target for marker-assisted selection or genome editing to create low-vicine/convicine faba bean cultivars by introducing loss-of-function vc1 alleles while preserving agronomic performance
Pathway_Genes.Potential_Tradeoffs: Possible impact on endogenous riboflavin-related metabolism is not fully resolved because VC1 is homologous to riboflavin-pathway enzymes; explicit yield penalties not established in this paper
Pathway_Genes.Applied_Promoters: LjUbi constitutive promoter for hairy-root overexpression
Pathway_Genes.Omics_Data: RNA-seq, de novo transcriptome assembly, metabolite profiling; NCBI BioProject PRJNA725986
Pathway_Genes.Other_Important_Info: VC1 was originally annotated as a riboflavin-biosynthetic bifunctional enzyme, but this study demonstrates recruitment to vicine biosynthesis. The low-vicine vc1 allele contains an AT insertion causing frameshift and likely loss of GTP cyclohydrolase II activity. Labeled GTP feeding in faba bean, narrow-leafed lupin, and bitter gourd supported a GTP-derived route for vicine/convicine-like compounds.
